title: C4OS Grill Question 004: Terminal Scope
description: This question defines what full interactive Terminal means for the first MVP. Submit the form, then paste the generated response back into chat.

questions.label: Who can type into the Terminal in MVP?
options: Agent only through approved command proposals
Both user and agent can write to the same terminal session
Separate terminals: user terminal and agent-owned command terminal
questions.help: Current proof supports backend-owned lifecycle. The important product choice is whether user input and agent command execution share one PTY or remain separate.

questions.label: What terminal commands can the agent run by default?
options: Commands inside active project when implied by the user's request
Any command inside active project after per-command approval
Any command on the machine after permission
No agent terminal commands in MVP
questions.help: This should align with the agent file authority decision.

questions.label: How should commands outside the active project work?
options: Allowed if user previously granted matching permission in chat
questions.help: This covers cwd outside the trusted project, commands that target outside paths, and shell operations with broader machine impact.

questions.label: What should C4OS record for terminal activity?
questions.help: Select what future users should be able to inspect after a run.

questions.label: Clarifications or constraints
questions.help: Add anything about user shell ownership, agent shell ownership, cancellation, secrets, environment variables, or non-macOS support.
